recipes.title: Mansaf
recipes.description: Jordan's national dish of lamb cooked in fermented dried yogurt jameed, served on rice and shrak bread with toasted nuts.
steps: Soak jameed overnight in warm water, then blend with the soaking water until smooth.
Simmer lamb with chopped onions, bay leaves, cardamom, and salt in 2 liters of water for 1.5 hours; skim foam.
Strain the broth and reserve the lamb separately.
Whisk the jameed liquid into the broth and simmer 30 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent breaking.
Return lamb to the jameed sauce and simmer 20 more minutes until tender and infused.
Cook rice with turmeric, ghee, and salt using the absorption method.
Toast almonds and pine nuts in ghee until golden.
Lay shrak on a large platter, top with rice, then lamb, ladle over jameed sauce, and finish with toasted nuts and parsley.

recipes.title: Maqluba
recipes.description: Levantine upside-down rice with chicken or lamb, fried eggplant, cauliflower, and potato, flipped at the table.
steps: Salt sliced eggplant and rest 30 minutes; pat dry.
Fry eggplant, cauliflower florets, and potato slices in batches until golden; drain.
Brown chicken with chopped onions, baharat, turmeric, and salt.
Add 1 liter water and simmer chicken 30 minutes; reserve broth.
Layer fried vegetables and chicken in a heavy pot, then top with sliced tomatoes.
Add washed rice and pour over enough hot broth to cover the rice by 2 cm.
Bring to a boil, then simmer covered on lowest heat for 35 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes, then place a serving platter on top and invert dramatically.

recipes.title: Musakhan
recipes.description: Palestinian-Jordanian roast chicken on flatbread piled with caramelized onions, sumac, and toasted pine nuts.
steps: Joint the chicken into 8 pieces and rub with olive oil, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and lemon juice.
Roast at 200C for 40 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Slice onions thinly and cook in 150 ml olive oil over low heat for 30 minutes until completely soft and translucent.
Stir sumac into the onions off the heat and season with salt.
Toast pine nuts in olive oil until golden.
Lay taboon bread on a tray, brush with olive oil from the chicken, and pile with the sumac onions.
Top each bread with a chicken piece and bake at 220C for 8 minutes to crisp.
Scatter pine nuts over the top and serve with extra lemon and yogurt.

recipes.title: Galayet Bandora
recipes.description: Quick Jordanian skillet of fresh tomatoes and garlic stewed in olive oil with chili, scooped up with bread.
steps: Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet and add sliced garlic; cook gently for 1 minute.
Stir in chopped chilies and cook for 30 seconds.
Add roughly chopped tomatoes, salt, and pepper.
Cook over medium-high heat for 15 minutes, smashing the tomatoes as they soften.
Reduce until the oil rises and the tomatoes are jammy.
Scatter chopped parsley and serve hot with warm flatbread for scooping.

recipes.title: Knafeh
recipes.description: Levantine pastry of stretchy white cheese under a crispy semolina crust soaked in rose-orange syrup.
steps: Boil sugar, water, and lemon juice for 10 minutes, then stir in rosewater and orange blossom water; chill.
Soak akkawi cheese in cold water for 1 hour to remove excess salt, changing the water twice.
Pulse the kataifi in a processor to chop fine, then toss with melted butter until evenly coated.
Press half the buttered pastry into a round shallow pan to form a tight base.
Layer drained akkawi and shredded mozzarella over the base, leaving a 1 cm border.
Top with the remaining pastry and press down firmly.
Cook on the stovetop over medium heat for 10 minutes until the bottom is deep golden, then bake at 200C for 10 minutes.
Invert onto a platter, pour the cold syrup over while hot, and shower with chopped pistachios.

recipes.title: Mujaddara
recipes.description: Levantine pilaf of brown lentils and rice topped with deeply caramelized onions; humble, vegan, and beloved.
steps: Rinse lentils and simmer in 750 ml water for 20 minutes until just tender; do not drain.
Slice onions thinly and cook in olive oil over medium heat for 30 minutes until deep brown and crisp at the edges.
Reserve half the onions for topping; keep the rest with their oil.
Add rinsed rice, cumin, allspice, salt, and pepper to the pot of lentils with their cooking water.
Top up with hot water so liquid covers the rice by 2 cm and stir in the onion oil.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 18 minutes until rice is tender.
Rest 10 minutes, fluff, and pile onto a platter with the reserved crispy onions on top.

recipes.title: Maftoul
recipes.description: Hand-rolled Palestinian-Jordanian giant couscous stewed with chicken, chickpeas, and warm spices.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown chicken pieces with sliced onions in olive oil until golden.
Add cinnamon, allspice, turmeric, salt, soaked chickpeas, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Steam the maftoul over the simmering broth in a colander or couscoussier for 30 minutes.
Toss the steamed maftoul with 2 tablespoons of broth and a drizzle of olive oil.
Mound the maftoul on a platter, top with chicken and chickpeas, and ladle over hot broth.

recipes.title: Mutabbal
recipes.description: Levantine smoked eggplant dip whipped with tahini, garlic, lemon, and yogurt, finished with olive oil and pomegranate.
steps: Char eggplants over an open flame until blackened all over and the flesh has collapsed.
Cool the eggplants in a colander to drain bitter juices, then peel and chop the smoked flesh.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 3 tablespoons cold water until pale and creamy.
Mash grated garlic into a paste with salt, then stir into the tahini.
Fold in the chopped eggplant and yogurt and beat until fluffy.
Spread on a plate and drizzle with olive oil; finish with pomegranate seeds and parsley.

recipes.title: Fattoush
recipes.description: Bright Levantine salad of crisp toasted flatbread, mixed greens, and vegetables tossed with sumac and pomegranate molasses.
steps: Tear pita into bite-size pieces and toast at 200C for 8 minutes until crisp.
Whisk lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, olive oil, sumac, and salt for the dressing.
Chop romaine, cucumber, tomatoes, radishes, and spring onions into bite-size pieces.
Toss the vegetables with chopped parsley and torn mint.
Add the toasted bread and dressing just before serving and toss to combine.
Finish with extra sumac and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Kibbeh
recipes.description: Levantine torpedo croquettes of bulgur and minced meat shells filled with spiced lamb and pine nuts, fried golden.
steps: Soak bulgur in cold water 30 minutes, then drain and squeeze dry.
Process bulgur with minced beef, half the onion, salt, and a pinch of allspice into a smooth paste.
Sauté the remaining chopped onion in 2 tablespoons oil, add lamb, and brown.
Stir in pine nuts, cinnamon, allspice, and salt; cool the filling completely.
Wet hands and form egg-size balls of the bulgur shell, hollow with a finger, fill with lamb, and seal into pointed torpedoes.
Fry in 180C oil for 5 minutes until deep golden.
Drain on paper and serve hot with yogurt or lemon.

recipes.title: Hummus
recipes.description: Silky Levantine puree of chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and garlic, swirled with olive oil and warm chickpeas on top.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight with baking soda and salt; drain.
Simmer chickpeas in fresh water for 1.5 hours until very tender, skimming foam.
Reserve 12 chickpeas and 200 ml cooking liquid; drain the rest.
Blend tahini with lemon juice and grated garlic until pale.
Add the warm chickpeas and salt and blend, streaming in cooking liquid until silky and fluffy.
Spread on a plate and create a well with the back of a spoon.
Drizzle with olive oil, top with reserved chickpeas, and dust with paprika and cumin.

recipes.title: Falafel
recipes.description: Levantine herbed chickpea fritters with parsley, cilantro, and cumin, deep fried to a crisp shell with a fluffy center.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight; drain thoroughly. Do not cook them.
Process chickpeas with onion, garlic, parsley, cilantro, cumin, coriander, and salt to a fine sandy mix.
Stir in baking soda and rest the mixture 30 minutes.
Shape into walnut-size patties with damp hands or a falafel mold.
Roll each patty in sesame seeds.
Heat oil to 180C and fry falafel in batches for 4 minutes until deep brown and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve in pita with tahini sauce, salad, and pickles.
